description: Declarative package-compatibility rules. Evaluated by compat-engine.ts during setup-build. When a rule's 'when' clause matches the project's package.json, the 'requires' clause is checked — a violation triggers either a fail (hard stop with corrective message) or a warn (advisory log). Wave 14 replaces the hard-coded check from Wave 13 Bug O.

rules.message: React {actual.when} requires @testing-library/react ^16.0.0 (got {actual.requires}). React 19 moved act() from react-dom/test-utils into react itself; testing-library <16 still calls the removed path and tests throw "React.act is not a function" in a loop. Bump @testing-library/react to ^16.0.0 in devDependencies, then re-run setup-build.

React 18 + @testing-library/react {actual.requires} — act() semantics differ from the newer testing-library 15+. Tests may be flaky; consider pinning @testing-library/react ^14.0.0 or upgrading to ^15.0.0 deliberately.
